title: Master Compound Engineering with Claude
description: Learn to build software where plans are code and AI handles implementation while you focus on strategy and taste.

cards.title: Welcome to Compound Engineering
cards.body: You'll learn the philosophy and practical workflow that transforms software development: treat plans as the new code, spend 80% of time planning, and let AI handle implementation while you steer with taste and strategy.

cards.title: The Compound Loop
cards.body: Compound Engineering creates a loop where every piece of work makes the next one easier. Your system learns your preferences, coding style, and domain knowledge, compounding speed with each iteration.

analogy.text: Like compound interest, each project deposits knowledge into your system that grows exponentially over time

cards.title: Install the Plugin
cards.body: Get the Compound Engineering plugin for Claude to access specialized commands for planning and building.
bullets: Copy the complete guide into your Claude conversation
Install the Compound Engineering plugin from Claude's plugin store

cards.title: Beliefs to Let Go
cards.body: Traditional software development habits that hold you back in the AI era:
bullets: Code must be written by hand — Let AI write it
Every line must be reviewed — Trust with safety nets
Solutions must originate from the engineer — Collaborate with AI
Writing code is the core job function — Planning is now core
cards.warn: Your ego might resist letting go of hand-coding. This is normal but necessary for compound growth.

cards.title: Philosophy Check
cards.question: In compound engineering, what becomes more important than writing code?
cards.correctFeedback: Exactly! Planning becomes your primary job function while AI handles implementation.
cards.wrongFeedback: Planning and brainstorming are now the core skills. AI can handle the coding.

cards.title: The 80/20 Rule
cards.body: Spend 80% of your time planning and 20% building. This feels backwards but creates better outcomes than traditional development ratios.
bullets: Only start building once the plan is solid

cards.title: The 50/50 Meta Rule
cards.body: For every hour on a feature, spend another hour on the meta layer — updating system prompts, docs, and style guides so AI compounds your preferences.

analogy.text: Like maintaining tools in a workshop — half your time builds the project, half keeps your tools sharp

cards.safe: This investment pays exponential dividends as your system learns your taste and patterns.

cards.title: Start with Brainstorming
cards.body: Before writing any code, run a brainstorm command to clarify exactly what you're building.
cards.codeCaption: This helps clarify requirements, edge cases, and user experience before planning technical implementation

cards.title: Generate Technical Plan
cards.body: After brainstorming, create a detailed technical plan. Plans are the new code — this is your primary deliverable.
bullets: Break down the feature into specific technical steps
Define data structures and API endpoints
Only proceed once the plan feels comprehensive

cards.title: Planning Mastery Unlocked
cards.body: You've learned the core principle: invest heavily in planning. Now let's make your environment agent-native so AI can execute these plans autonomously.

cards.title: Agent-Native Environment
cards.body: Make your stack AI-friendly by adding CLIs for every action you currently do manually — deploy, debug, customer support lookup.
bullets: Replace GUI workflows with command-line interfaces
Create scripts for common debugging tasks
Enable AI to execute without human intervention
cards.warn: Never give AI access to production databases or destructive operations without safeguards.

cards.title: Build Your CLI Arsenal
cards.body: Add command-line interfaces for every manual task so your AI agent can execute them.
helpItems: Use existing tools like Vercel CLI, Railway CLI
Write custom scripts for domain-specific tasks

cards.title: Trust with Safety Nets
cards.body: Build trust in AI by creating safety nets rather than micromanaging. Use monitoring, tests, and staged deployments instead of line-by-line reviews.
cards.safe: Trust grows over time as you see AI consistently deliver quality work within your safety framework.

cards.title: Safety Check
cards.question: How should you handle concerns about AI code quality?

options.text: Review every line of code manually
Only use AI for simple tasks

cards.correctFeedback: Perfect! Safety nets scale better than manual reviews and build sustainable trust.
cards.wrongFeedback: Manual reviews don't scale. Build automated safety nets like tests and monitoring instead.

cards.title: The Development Stages
cards.body: Most developers progress through these stages of AI adoption:
cards.safe: Each stage requires relearning development habits. This is normal and necessary.

cards.title: Implement the Review Loop
cards.body: After each AI-generated PR, compare results against your original plan to identify gaps and improve future planning.
bullets: Review the PR against the technical plan
Note what was missed or misunderstood
Update your planning templates with learnings
Refine system prompts based on patterns

source.author: Every
source.description: PR review and system improvement process

cards.title: Extract Your Taste
cards.body: The compound loop works by extracting your preferences into the system — your coding style, UI choices, architecture patterns, and what makes you happy as a developer.
bullets: Document your preferred libraries (TailwindCSS, ShadCN, etc.)

cards.title: Apply the 50/50 Rule
cards.body: For every hour spent building a feature, spend another hour improving your meta-system so future builds compound faster.
bullets: Update system prompts with new learnings
Add new CLI tools or shortcuts
helpItems: Keep a learning log for each project
Create templates for common feature types
cards.troubleshoot: If this feels like overhead, remember: this investment pays exponential returns as your system learns.

cards.title: Parallelization Power
cards.body: Lean into AI's ability to work on multiple aspects simultaneously. Unlike humans, AI can handle parallel workstreams without context switching costs.
bullets: Work on frontend and backend simultaneously
Update documentation in parallel with code

cards.title: Workflow Check
cards.question: What's the ideal time split in compound engineering?
cards.correctFeedback: Right! Heavy planning investment creates better outcomes and leverages AI's strengths.
cards.wrongFeedback: The magic ratio is 80% planning, 20% building — this maximizes AI effectiveness.

cards.title: Start Your First Compound Project
cards.body: Put it all together: brainstorm → plan → build → review → improve the system.
bullets: Choose a small feature to practice with
Spend 80% of time on brainstorming and planning
Review plan vs. results and update your system
cards.code: brainstorm: Simple todo app with priority levels
cards.codeCaption: Start small to build confidence in the compound engineering process

cards.title: Compound Engineer Unlocked!
cards.body: You've mastered the philosophy and workflow of compound engineering. Remember: plans are the new code, invest in your meta-system, and trust AI with proper safety nets. Each project will now make the next one faster and better.
